titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the George Meredith Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid prepared according to Describing Archives: A Content Standard (DACS).

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday January 28, 2020 at 04:01

did.unittitle: George Meredith Collection

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains manuscripts, annotated proofs and printed works, correspondence, and other papers documenting the life and works of George Meredith. Contents include a complete set of the Chapman and Hall edition of Meredith's Works, extensively interleaved with corrections and annotations in the author's hand; the autograph manuscript of The adventures of Harry Richmond (1871); and over 200 letters and transcripts of letters, including 26 to Frederic Chapman, 17 to Theodore Watts-Dunton, and 45 to Augustus Jessopp.The collection contains manuscripts, annotated proofs and printed works, correspondence, and other papers documenting the life and works of George Meredith. Contents include a complete set of the Chapman and Hall edition of Meredith's Works, extensively interleaved with corrections and annotations in the author's hand; the autograph manuscript of The adventures of Harry Richmond (1871); and over 200 letters and transcripts of letters, including 26 to Frederic Chapman, 17 to Theodore Watts-Dunton, and 45 to Augustus Jessopp.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The George Meredith Collection contains items acquired through gift or purchase from various sources, many from Frank Altschul or purchased on the Frank Altschul Fund. Provenance information is contained in notes under individual items.

accessrestrict.head: Information about Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Ownership & Copyright
userestrict.p: The George Meredith Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Cite As
prefercite.p: George Meredith Collection. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Notes
processinfo.p: Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from an initial survey of the contents and compiled from individual card catalogs. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those given by library staff at the time of acquisition. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during basic processing.

This collection includes materials previously identified by the following call numbers: Uncat MS Vault Shelves Meredith; MS Vault Shelves Meredith; Uncat MS Vault Files Meredith; MS Vault 13:4; Uncat MS Vault 87

bioghist.head: George Meredith, 1828-1909
bioghist.p: George Meredith (1828-1909), English novelist and poet. His novels include The Ordeal of Richard Feverel (1859), The Egoist (1879), and Diana of the Crossways (1885). His sonnet sequence depicting the breakdown of an unhappy marriage, Modern Love (1862), was thought to be based on his own unhappy first marriage to Mary Peacock Nicholls (1821-1861).

scopecontent.head: Description of the Papers
scopecontent.p: The George Meredith collection contains manuscripts, annotated proofs and printed works, correspondence, and other papers documenting the life and works of George Meredith. Contents include a complete set of the Chapman and Hall edition of Meredith's Works, extensively interleaved with corrections and annotations in the author's hand; the autograph manuscript of The adventures of Harry Richmond (1871); and over 200 letters and transcripts of letters, including 26 to Frederic Chapman, 17 to Theodore Watts-Dunton, and 45 to Augustus Jessopp.

did.unittitle: George Meredith's Works: Interleaved
Diana of the Crossways, Volume I

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chapman and Hall's edition, interleaved with quarto sheets in preparation for Constable's Edition de Luxe. 1896. Manuscript corrections throughout text and on quarto sheets mostly in the author's hand.

did.unittitle: Diana of the Crossways, Volume II

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chapman and Hall's edition, interleaved with quarto sheets in preparation for Constable's Edition de Luxe. 1896. Manuscript corrections throughout text and on quarto sheets mostly in the author's hand.

did.unittitle: Diana of the Crossways, Volume III

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chapman and Hall's edition, interleaved with quarto sheets in preparation for Constable's Edition de Luxe. 1896. Manuscript corrections throughout text and on quarto sheets mostly in the author's hand.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chapman and Hall's edition, interleaved with quarto sheets in preparation for Constable's Edition de Luxe. 1896. Manuscript corrections throughout text and on quarto sheets mostly in the author's hand.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chapman and Hall's edition, interleaved with quarto sheets in preparation for Constable's Edition de Luxe. 1896. Manuscript corrections throughout text and on quarto sheets mostly in the author's hand.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With autographed portrait of George Meredith.

Chapman and Hall's edition, interleaved with quarto sheets in preparation for Constable's Edition de Luxe. 1896. Manuscript corrections throughout text and on quarto sheets mostly in the author's hand.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chapman and Hall's edition, interleaved with quarto sheets in preparation for Constable's Edition de Luxe. 1896. Manuscript corrections throughout text and on quarto sheets mostly in the author's hand.

did.unittitle: Writings: Poetry Collections and Longer Works
Modern Love

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proofs, with manuscript corrections throughout the text in the author's hand.

did.unittitle: Ballads and Poems of the Tragic Life

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proofs, with manuscript corrections throughout the text in the author's hand.

did.unittitle: The Empty Purse, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proofs, with manuscript corrections throughout the text in the author's hand.

did.unittitle: Writings: Individual Texts
Poems by George Meredith
Cleopatra : autograph manuscript poem in the handwriting of his wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With a penciled note by a son of GM on page 1: "This is my mother's handwriting - it is a poem by my father - refer to works."

did.unittitle: Creed : original autograph manuscript, signed at end with initials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bound in red morocco, with an introduction and transcription.

did.unittitle: The Crisis : autograph manuscript poem signed G.M.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In green cloth case with gold lettering on front cover.

did.unittitle: Earth and a Wedded Woman : original autograph manuscript
Empedocles : autograph manuscript poem

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In green cloth folder, with gold lettering on the front cover.

did.unittitle: England before the storm : autograph manuscript, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Title in pencil, numerous corrections throughout.

did.unittitle: Events in a Rustic Mirror : autograph manuscript poem, apparently unpublished

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by a portrait of Meredith etched and signed by S. Hollyer.
Bound in full dark blue morocco.
Includes a typed letter, signed, from William Maxse Meredith to Frank Hollings, 1916 November 28.

did.unittitle: A Faith on Trial : autograph manuscript poem, heavily revised by author, and Change in Recurrence , autograph manuscript poem

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mounted on Japon vellum and bound in three-quarter dark blue morocco.

did.unittitle: Fall'n is Lucifer! : autograph manuscript poem, 3 stanzas of 6 lines each.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From the estate of Gabriel Wells, 1947.

did.unittitle: Hard Weather : autograph manuscript poem
Hungarian Air : autograph manuscript poem
Jane the Jumper : autograph manuscript of an early version of part of the poem Jump-to-Glory Jane

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift of Halsted Van der Poel, 1941.

did.unittitle: Jump-to-Glory Jane : original autograph manuscript, signed at top of the first page

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Laid in dark blue morocco portfolio.

did.unittitle: The Longest Day : autograph manuscript of two stanzas of the poem

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is an early draft (incomplete) of the poem.

did.unittitle: Meditation Under Stars : autograph manuscript poem

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In blue cloth folder with gilt lettering.

did.unittitle: Meditation Under Stars : autograph manuscript poem
The Night Walk : unsigned typescript, with manuscript corrections and page proof
Ode to Colonel Charles (Dying General C. B. B.) : autograph manuscript poem

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In green cloth folder, with gold lettering on the front cover.

did.unittitle: Outer and Inner : original autograph manuscript with corrections in the author's hand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inlaid with printed title-page and a printed portrait of Meredith at 80.

did.unittitle: Outside the Crowd : proofsheet and corresponding page from the "National Review" for September 1896 in which it was first published; The Appeasement of Demeter : autograph manuscript of stanza IX, and page proof of the whole poem as set up for "MacMillan's Magazine" for September 1887; and Mother to Babe : proofsheet as set up for the "English Illustrated Magazine" for October 1886 in which the poem was first published, and the corresponding page from the magazine
Proofs of Poems. (Binder's title)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poems. Edinburgh, R. & R. Clark, Printers, 1894.
Contains poems from Poems and lyrics of the joy of earth , 1883; Ballads and poems of tragic life , 1887; and Modern love and poems of the English roadside , 1862.

did.unittitle: The Riddle for Men : autograph manuscript poem

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In green cloth folder, with gold lettering on front cover.

did.unittitle: The Sage Enamoured and the Honest Lady : original autograph manuscript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With four short poems which follow it in the 1892 edition of Modern Love , also five pages of an earlier draft.
Laid in is a detailed description of the manuscript in an unidentified hand.
In an olive green morocco case.

did.unittitle: Scattered Poems : typed copies of various poems

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From different magazines, corrected by the author for Volume 3 of the edition de luxe of his collected works.

did.unittitle: Seed-Time : autograph manuscript poem
The South-Wester : autograph manuscript poem

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A complete early draft of this poem together with an earlier draft, each containing many alterations and corrections.

did.unittitle: The South-Wester : autograph manuscript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A complete final draft of the poem.

did.unittitle: A Tragedy of Modern Love : autograph manuscript poem, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift of Frank Altschul and the Library Associates, 1965.

did.unittitle: 'Twas Yesterday Such a Black Day : autograph manuscript poem, signed
The Vital Choice and With the Huntress : autograph manuscript poems

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bound in full stiff vellum, incorrectly gold-lettered on front cover: "Manuscript. The Test of Manhood. George Meredith."

did.unittitle: The Wild Rose : autograph manuscript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With additional pages of incomplete manuscripts, notes, etc.

did.unittitle: Prose by George Meredith
The Adventures of Harry Richmond : autograph manuscript, on loose sheets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box one of two blue boxes.

did.unittitle: The Adventures of Harry Richmond : autograph manuscript, on loose sheets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box two of two blue boxes.

did.unittitle: The Amazing Marriage : Cole Manuscript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Yale owns a portion of three versions of this novel: Nicholls manuscript, Cole manuscript, later version.

did.unittitle: The Amazing Marriage : Nicholls Manuscript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Yale owns a portion of three versions of this novel: Nicholls manuscript, Cole manuscript, later version.

did.unittitle: The Amazing Marriage : Later Manuscript; and The Parting of the Hour : autograph manuscript poem of 4 lines

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Yale owns a portion of three versions of this novel: Nicholls manuscript, Cole manuscript, later version.
The Parting of the Hour appears on the verso of page 387.

did.unittitle: Armaments : autograph manuscript, corrected, incomplete
Celt and Saxon : autograph manuscript signed
Diana of the Crossways : portion of the original manuscript
Diana of the Crossways : fragment of page 486 of the manuscript, half page

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Laid in are photostats of pages 580 and 586 of the manuscript from the Pierpont Morgan Library 1.
Gift of John W. Warrington (Library Associates). July, 1936.

did.unittitle: Disarmament : original manuscript prose statement

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Opening line: "Ideals, after a long period of derision, are at last matter for belief..."

did.unittitle: The Egoist : autograph manuscript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift of Arthur A. Houghton, Jr., May 1966.

did.unittitle: The Egoist : autograph and typewritten manuscript of the dramatic version

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arranged for the stage by George Meredith and Alfred Sutro.

In a green morocco case. Laid in the case with the manuscript is a typed, signed letter from John Bronson to Andrew Leogh, regarding the incorrect binding of The Egoist by George Meredith, 1931 October 24.

did.unittitle: An Essay on Comedy : printed with manuscript annotations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: On the idea of comedy, and of the uses of the comic spirit. In the New Quarterly Magazine. London, 1877.
Manuscript corrections throughout text in the author's hand.

Bound with An Essay on Comedy are "A lecture delivered at the London Institution, February 1st, 1877"; Charles Merivale's undated " Homer's Iliad in English rhymed verse "; a review from Fortnightly Review, 1869 May; Frederic H. Myers' undated "Saint-Paul: a poem" ; and a review from Fortnightly Review, 1868 January.

did.unittitle: The Friend of an Engaged Couple : original autograph manuscript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes publisher's slip, printed form filled in with author, title, 1862, and in one corner the name and address of John Lane, Esq.
In red half morocco slip case.

did.unittitle: Lord Ormont and His Aminta : portion of the original manuscript
Mrs. Meynell's Two Books of Essays : autograph manuscript signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Review of The Rhythm of Life and The Colour of Life .
In a green cloth folder with gold lettering on the front cover.

did.unittitle: One of Our Conquerors : original autograph manuscript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Filed with the manuscript is an autograph letter, signed, to Augustus Jessopp, 1890 May 30.

did.unittitle: One of Our Conquerors : autograph manuscript of the final draft
Queries : original autograph manuscript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written by John Lane concerning George Meredith's works, with replies in Meredith's autograph.
In a green half morocco slip case.

did.unittitle: Rhoda Fleming : a dramatization of the novel by A. Phillipson, typescript copy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by a programme of the production by the Playwrights Theatre in London at the Ambassadors Theatre on March 21, 1921 and a group of newspaper reviews of the play.
Accompanied also by a typed letter, signed, from the producer Irene Hentschel, to an unidentified recipient, transmitting the copy of the play.

did.unittitle: The Sentimentalists. A Comedy : autograph manuscript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With a typescript of a later version with alterations in Sir James Barrie's hand.

did.unittitle: The Tragic Comedians : autograph manuscript with signature.
Letters
Bound Albums of Letters
Autograph Letters of George Meredith

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The following are included in the volume:
Autograph letter, signed, to an unidentified recipient, possibly W. C. Bonaparte Wyse, [1870 April 20].
Autograph letter, signed, to an unidentified recipient, possibly W. C. Bonaparte Wyse, 1876 May 20.
Autograph letter, signed, to George Bainton, 1888 February 1.
Basselin, Olivier, 15th century: "From the Chants du Vaudevire , par Olivier Basselin. vaudevire 6th"; manuscript copy, in George Meredith's hand, of 5 stanzas of 4 lines each, undated.
Autograph letter, signed, to Arthur Cecil, 1881 May 17.
3 autograph letters, signed, to Frederic Chapman, 1864 October 10, 1864 November 9, 1865 July 21.

Autograph letter, signed, to Frederic Chapman, with a manuscript note by Mr. Chapman, signed with his initials, on verso of envelope, explaining his gift of this letter to Mr. W. H. Dodge (?) of Manchester, 1901 June 13.
Autograph letter, signed, to Wilfrid Hugh Chesson, 1897 September 11.
4 autograph letters, signed, to Mr. Colles, 1893 February 7, 1893 February 15, 1893 August 15, undated.
Autograph letter, signed, to Ella Hepworth Dixon, 1909 May 13.
Autograph letter, signed, to Havelock Ellis, 1886 December 31.
Autograph letter, signed, to Frank Harris, 1898 July 25.
Autograph letter, signed, to [Violet] Hunt, 1908 February 26.
Autograph letter, signed, to [Norman] MacColl, 1883 February 1.
Autograph letter, signed, to St. B. [James Augustus Cotter Morison], 1886 July 12.
Post card to Mr. E. W. Muller, [1900 October 29].
Autograph note, signed, to Robert Orridge, undated.
Autograph letter, signed, to [John William] Parker, [circa 1856].
Autograph letter, signed, to York Powell, 1893 September 12.
3 autograph letters, signed, to Mark André Raffalovich, 1884 March 5, 1884 October 23, 1885 April 28.
Autograph note, signed, to Mr. Reeves, 1891 January 10.
2 autograph letters, signed, to Leitch Ritchie, 1849[?] June 12, 1849 December 1.
Autograph letter, signed, to George Stevenson, 1886 November 7.
3 autograph letters, signed, to "Glasgowgo," i.e. George Stevenson, 1891 June 16, 1892 February 5, November 2.
Autograph letter, signed, to Mrs. George Stevenson, 1889 January 4.
Autograph letter, signed, to "Callandria," i.e. Mrs. George Stevenson, 1894 August 21.
Postcard, signed with initials and written in French, to Mrs. George Stevenson, [1898 March 11].
Autograph letter, signed, to Algernon Charles Swinburne, [1866] March 12.
Autograph letter, signed, to Mrs. Seymour Trower, undated.
Autograph letter, signed, to Mrs. Seymour Trower, 1889 January 21.
Autograph letter, signed, to "the Gondolier" [i.e. Seymour Trower], 1893 November 17.
Autograph letter, signed, to Lady BytheWey [i.e. Mrs. Seymour Trower], 1898 December 14.
2 autograph letters, signed, to "the Gondolier" [i.e. Seymour Trower], 1905 July 31, August 2.

Töpffer, Rodolphe: Nouvelles Genevoises : manuscript copy, in George Meredith's hand, with copy of two paragraphs, in George Meredith's hand, one headed "A Boy's Studies" (p. 118) and the other from p. 287, undated.
5 autograph letters, signed, to W. C. Bonaparte Wyse, 1863 January 7, 1876 October 6, [1879 June], 1881 January 5.
Autograph letter, signed, to Mrs. Wyse, 1882 January 23.

did.unittitle: George Meredith to Various Correspondents, Volume I

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume I of II, in red cloth case.
The following are included in Volume I:
2 autograph letters, signed, to John W. Parker, 1850 December 12, 1855 July 8.
Autograph letter, signed, to James Vizetelly, [1851].
3 autograph letters, signed, to Samuel Lucas, [1860], 1861 June 12, [1861].
2 autograph letters, signed, to Edward Walford, 1862 January 5, [1862].
Autograph letter, signed, to Augustus Jessopp, [1866] May 18.
Autograph letter, signed, to Frederick A. Sandys, 1869 May 25.
Autograph letter, signed, to Moncure D. Conway, 1874 June 18.
2 autograph letters, signed, to Frederic Chapman, 1882 August 2, 1886 July 6.
2 autograph letters, signed, to George Stevenson, 1886 November 17, 1887 April 30.
Autograph letter, signed, to George Bainton, 1887 September 14.

did.unittitle: George Meredith to Various Correspondents, Volume II

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume II of II, in red cloth case.
The following are included in Volume II:
5 autograph letters, signed, to John Lane, 1890 June 8, 1891 May 27, 1892 August 6, 1894 June 22, 1894 December 23.
Autograph letter, signed, to Richard Le Gallienne, 1890 December 9.
4 autograph letters, signed, to Frederic Chapman, 1891 June 11, 1891 November 19, 1892 June 14, 1892 June 22.
Autograph letter, signed, to Frederick A. Sandys, 1892 April 7.
2 reports on publisher's reading, one with heading, dated 1894 December 3, the other without place or date.

did.unittitle: George Meredith Letters, typed copies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Probably the companion to "George Meredith to Various Correspondents"
In a half green morocco case.
The following are included in the volume:
Typescript copy of letter, signed, to George Bainton, 1887 September 14.
Typescript copies, in duplicate, of 6 letters, signed, to Frederic Chapman, 1882 August 2 - 1892 June 22.
Typescript copy of letter, signed, to Augustus Jessopp, [1866] May 18.
9 typescripts to John Lane (8 letters typed in duplicate), 1889 May 5 - 1896 June 2.
Typescript copies, in duplicate, of 32 letters to Samuel Lucas, [1859?]-1863.
Typescript copy, in duplicate, of letter probably addressed to Samuel Lucas, enclosing the songs for Once a Week , [1859] June 4.
Typescript copy, in duplicate, of letter, signed, to James Cotter Morison, 1886 February 11.
Typescript copy, in duplicate, of letter, signed, to John W. Parker, 1855 July 8.
Typescript copy, in duplicate, of letter, signed, to Tom [Taylor?], 1859.
Typescript copies, in duplicate, of 2 letters, signed, to [William?] Tinsley, [1865], undated.
Typescript copy, in duplicate, of letter, signed, to James Vizetelly, [1851].
Typescript copy, in duplicate, of a letter to Edward Walford, 1892 January 5.
Typescript copy, in duplicate, of a letter to Edward Walford, [1862?].

did.unittitle: Letters of George Meredith collected and edited by his son. 2nd Edition. M. Buxton Forman's copy with manuscript annotations. Volume I.
Letters of George Meredith collected and edited by his son. 2nd Edition. M. Buxton Forman's copy with manuscript annotations. Volume II.
Clippings, photos, letters, and other manuscript material removed from Letters of George Meredith collected and edited by his son, Volume I.
Clippings, photos, letters, and other manuscript material removed from Letters of George Meredith collected and edited by his son, Volume II.
Clippings, photos, letters, and other manuscript material removed from Letters of George Meredith collected and edited by his son, Volume I and II.
Bound Letters to Individuals
26 autograph letters, signed, to Frederic Chapman, on literary matters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bound in brown morocco, in brown linen box.

did.unittitle: 16 autograph letters, signed, to Theodore Watts-Dunton

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three are written in the hand of Marie E. Meredith (Mrs. Sturgis), one of which is signed by Meredith and one by her with her own name.
Also includes two typewritten letters, signed, from William Maxse Meredith to Theodore Watts-Dunton, 1909 August 4 and 13.

did.unittitle: 6 autograph letters, signed, and 2 autograph notes, signed, to G. W. Foote

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes an undated note in an unidentified hand enclosing £2 to the "Freethinker" and two autograph notes, signed to George Ward, 1894 June 7 and 13, enclosing his contribution to the testimonial for G. W. Foote.

did.unittitle: 24 autograph letters, signed, to Mr. & Mrs. William Hardman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by an original photograph of George Meredith.
In a green half morocco case.
Also includes a typewritten letter, signed, from Frank Altschul to Professor Chauncey B. Tinker of Davenport College, 1935 May 16.

did.unittitle: 45 autograph letters, signed, to Canon Augustus Jessopp, Volume I

fileplan.head: File Plan
fileplan.p: With a manuscript preface of 7 pages consisting of biographical sketches of Meredith and Jessopp from the Encyclopedia Britannica .
One letter (1905), with an envelope endorsed in an unidentified hand, was dictated to Marie Sturgis.

did.unittitle: 45 autograph letters, signed, to Canon Augustus Jessopp, Volume II

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With a manuscript preface of 7 pages consisting of biographical sketches of Meredith and Jessopp from the Encyclopedia Britannica .
One letter (1905), with an envelope endorsed in an unidentified hand, was dictated to Marie Sturgis.

did.unittitle: 18 autograph letters, signed, to Mrs. Walter Palmer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mounted and enclosed in a half-morocco case.

did.unittitle: Autograph letter, signed, to Frances Forbes Robertson
11 autograph letters, signed, to Mrs. Janet A. Ross

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by fragments of verse, a letter from Mrs. Ross to Dr. Jessopp (August 19, 1887), a description of the collection by Buxton Forman, an autograph letter, signed, from Arthur G. Meredith to Mrs. Ross, dated [1861] September 25, with a note at the end from George Meredith, dated [1861] October 8.

title.part: "The waves are pressing up with force"
"The chambers of my heart are many, dear"
"Night walks on the earth with silver feet"
"Tis midnight in the skies, my dear"

scopecontent.p: Also bound with the letters are: Letters from Egypt : typewritten manuscript of the introduction of Lady Duff Gordon's "Letters from Egypt", enclosed with letter of August 27, 1901 with corrections in Meredith's hand; Schubert's "Farewell" : autograph manuscript verse, undated, accompanied by typescript; and an undated fragment, possibly from a letter, of a scene between Meredith and Maurice Fitzgerald, accompanied by typescript.

did.unittitle: 2 autograph letters, signed, to Robert Louis Stevenson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mounted in volume bound in dark blue leather.

did.unittitle: Autograph letter, signed, to W. C. Bonaparte Wyse

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brief biographical sketch of Meredith, typewritten.
One portrait and one caricature of Meredith.
Bound in brown morocco, with bookplate of John L. Clawson.

did.unittitle: Loose-Leaf Letters to Individuals
Letter, signed, to unidentified recipient
Autograph letter, signed, to an unidentified recipient

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Advising her not to publish her poems.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by envelope and typewritten transcript.

did.unittitle: Autograph letter, signed, to "My dear bridesmaid Bessie"
Autograph letter, signed, to Guy Mortimer Carleton

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift of Mrs. Guy M. Carleton, 1939.

did.unittitle: Autograph letter, signed, to Mrs. Edward [Chapman]
Autograph letter, signed, to Frederick Chapman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is an early letter in which Meredith speaks of important publishing matters, including proofs of a book on Japan. It is also one of the few letters in which he speaks of his wife without bitterness. (There is no reference to her in 1852, and they were subsequently divorced after a very unhappy marriage.)
Accompanied by two typed letters from William M. Meredith identifying the recipient. 1909 August 6 and 13.

did.unittitle: Autograph letter, signed, to Frederic Chapman
Autograph letter, signed, to Fred [Chapman]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by an autograph letter, signed, from Marie Meredith to Mrs. Wyse, 1870 May 16.

did.unittitle: Autograph letter, signed, to Fred [Chapman]
Autograph letter, signed, to Chapman and Hall
2 autograph letters, signed, to Edith Nicolls Clarke
10 autograph letters, signed, to [Edward] Clodd

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1904 April 18 (Attached is a printed letter of his to which he refers).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Meredith sends a check for books for the Club [the Browning Society?] and declines an invitation to become President.

did.unittitle: 2 autograph letter, signed, to John Lane

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mentioning Diana of the Crossways and R. Le Gallienne.

did.unittitle: 5 autograph letters, signed, to Samuel Lawrence
Autograph letter, signed, to Professor Long

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Laid in: an invitation to a party at Windsor Castle, 1907 June 22 and two checks, 1872 December 26 and 1873 January 15.

did.unittitle: Autograph letter, signed, to the editor of Once a Week [Samuel Lucas]
23 autograph letters, signed, to Samuel Lucas, editor of Once a Week

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mentioning some of Meredith's early works: Evan Harrington , etc., and also "All the year around" , Wilkie Collins & "The Woman in White" , Dickens, Thackeray, and "Vanity Fair" , Rossetti and "Cassandra" , Swinburne and "East Lynne" ; and giving some very interesting criticism.

did.unittitle: Autograph letter, signed, to S[amuel] Lucas.
Autograph letter, signed, to the Reverend James McKechnie

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: About his novel The Shaving of Shagpat .

did.unittitle: 13 autograph letters, signed, to Louison Macpherson (later Mrs. Morton)
9 autograph letters, signed, to Violet Maxse

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an autograph letter, signed, from C. Hubert Parry to "Sue", 1906 March 16.

did.unittitle: Autograph letter, signed, to Henry Maxwell
2 autograph letters, signed, to Mortimer Menpes
30 autograph letters, signed, to his son, William Maxse Meredith

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 9 of the letters have no year.
The letter of April 7 [1903] beginning "Box Hill violets" is addressed to Mrs. W. M. Meredith.

did.unittitle: 87 autograph letters and notes to his son, William Maxse Meredith

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a Dumfries Burns Club circular, 1896, with an autograph note by George Meredith on page 4.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Autograph letter, signed, to William R[ossetti]
Autograph letter, signed, to W. M. Rossetti

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In praise of Dante Gabriel Rossetti and his appreciation of the merits of young writers, "one of the few instances in which he surpassed even Swinburne."

Letters by Others
Barrett-Lennard, Sir Thomas: autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Benner, Jean: autograph letter, signed, to [George Meredith]
Blunt, Arthur Cecil: autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Blunt, Arthur Cecil: autograph letter, signed, to George Meredith
Blunt, Wilfrid Scawen: autograph letter, signed, to [George Meredith]
Brackenburg, Major-General Charles Booth: autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Calthrop, John Alfred (Clayton): autograph letter, signed, to George Meredith
Chapman, Frederic: autograph letter, signed, to Roberts Brothers
Chapman, Frederic: 11 autograph letters, signed, to William Morris Colles
Chapman & Hall: 2 autograph letters, signed, to William Morris Colles
Deakin, Alfred: autograph letter, signed, to George Meredith
Ellis, Stewart March: typed letter, signed, to Frank Altschul
Forman, Maurice Buxton: autograph letter, signed, to [Edward Clodd]
Frith, Walter: autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Gilkes, Arthur Herman: autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Girdlestone, Gertrude: autograph letter, signed, to H. Buxton Forman
Godkin, [James]: autograph letter, signed, to George Meredith (fragment)
Gosse, Sir Edmund William: autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Greenwood, Frederick Francis: autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Greenwood, Frederick Francis: autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Halkett, George Roland: autograph letter, signed, to William Morris Colles
Harrison, Frederic: autograph letter, signed, to [William Maxse] Meredith
Harrison, Frederic: autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Hill, Frank Harrison: autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Hogg, Thomas Jefferson: 4 lettters to T. L. Peacock and 10 to Mary Ellen Peacock copied by H. B. Forman
Holzmann, Maurice: autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Inglish, Douglas: autograph letter, signed, to [George Meredith]
Ingram, John: autograph letter, signed, to [William Maxse] Meredith
Jacks, Lawrence Pearsall: autograph letter, signed, to [William Maxse Meredith]
Keene, Charles: autograph letter, signed, to Joseph Swain

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With an original drawing for Once a Week.

did.unittitle: Mackail, John William: autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Macmillan & Company: autograph letter, signed, to Roberts Brothers
Meredith, Marie E.: autograph letter, signed, to William Morris Colles
Meredith, Marie E.: autograph letter, signed, to an unidentified recipient

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written on behalf of her father

did.unittitle: Meredith, Mary Ellen (Peacock) Nicholls: autograph letter, signed, to Henry Wallis
Meredith, Mary Ellen (Peacock) Nicholls: envelope addressed to Henry Wallis
Meredith, William Maxse: 2 typed letters, signed, to M. Buxton Forman
Millais, John Everett: autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Morison, James Cotter: autograph letter, signed, to Mrs. [Marie] Meredith
Morley, John: autograph letter, signed, to Mrs. [Marie] Meredith
Morley, John: autograph letter, signed, to Mrs. [Marie] Meredith
Nalz, J.: autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Palgrave, William Gifford: autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Partridge, Sir Bernard: autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Prichard, Hamlin: autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Sandys, Frederick Augustus: autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Seebohm, Frederic T.: autograph letter, signed, to George Meredith
Sonon: autograph letter, signed, to [George Meredith]
Steele, Anna Caroline (Wood): autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Stephen, Sir Leslie: autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Sturgis, Marie Meredith: autograph letter, signed, to an unidentified recipient
Sturgis, Marie Meredith: autograph letter, signed, to "Dear Madam."
Sudermann, Hermann: autograph letter, signed, to [George Meredith]
Watts, George Frederick: autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Wilkinson, Henry Spenser: autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Wolseley, Sir Garnet Joseph, Viscount and Baron: typed letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Wood, A. M.: autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Wood, Emma Caroline (Michell): autograph letter, signed, to [George] Meredith
Various persons: 5 letters, signed, to and about George Meredith
Other Papers
Bibliography of the Works of George Meredith: Original Autograph Letters of Various Correspondents to John Lane, Volume I.
Bibliography of the Works of George Meredith: Original Autograph Letters of Various Correspondents to John Lane, Volume II.
Bibliography of the Works of George Meredith: Original Autograph Letters of Various Correspondents to John Lane, Volume III.
Epigrams and apophthegms later used in Meredith's novels on the inside back cover of Hubert Ries' Violin-Schule für den ersten Unterricht... (Leipzig [1840])
Fragments of the Iliad in English in hexameter verse: manuscript, printer's copy, with correctinos by George Meredith.
"Meredith's character," in an unidentified hand, stamped; "Marrell," manuscript, in an unidentified hand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by two autograph letters, signed, by George Meredith to unidentified recipients, undated, and 3 clippings.

did.unittitle: Miscellanea.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Laid in red cloth box with title: George Meredith. Miscellanea.
The following are included in the collection:
On the Danger of War : typescript of a poem, published in the Pall Mall Gazette , 1885 May 1.
To the Cardinal Manning : typescript of a poem, published in the Pall Mall Gazette , 1885 November 5.

did.unittitle: Notebook I: red quarto notebook containing fair copies of poems

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including some translated from the German, fragments of verse, lists of titles, and personal memoranda.
Loose leaves from this notebook are collected in Notebook 10A.

did.unittitle: Notebook II: blue quarto notebook

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a long early poem, "Wandering Willie," an incomplete story, "The Land of Flowers," an essay on Grillparzer, which formed the basis for Meredith's article "Austrian poets" in Fraser's Magazine (August 1852) and fragments and memoranda.
Loose leaves from this notebook are collected in Notebook 10A.

did.unittitle: Notebooks 1-10, 10A, A &D: autograph notebooks containing sketches, aphorisms, and drafts of poetry and prose.
Notes as a publisher's reader of Chapman and Hall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original manuscript notes made by George Meredith as publisher's reader for Chapman and Hall, giving opinions of over 400 books. The foolscap sheets were furnished by the publisher, bearing authors and titles in an unidentified hand, with spaces left for the reader's notes and dated August 10, 1889 - September 17, 1895. With brown paper envelope, torn, endorsed by Meredith.

did.unittitle: Original contracts with Chapman and Hall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Laid in half green levant case, gold tooled.
The following are included in the case:

Original manuscript agreement between Meredith and Chapman & Hall for the puiblication, for 7 years from the dates of publication respectively, of Richard Feverel , Emilia in England , Vittoria , Rhoda Fleming , and Beauchamp's Career , 1885 June 2.

Original manuscript agreement, for 6 years from the date of publication, between Meredith and Chapman & Hall for the publication of a 3 volume edition for the periodical pubcliation of One of our Conquerors , 1890 July 10.
Original manuscript agreement between Meredith and Chapman & Hall for the publication of certain of Meredith's novels for a period of 3 years, 1893 July 19.
Original manuscript agreement between Meredith and Chapman & Hall for the publication of a 3 volume and a 1 volume edition of Lord Ormont and his Aminta , 1894 April 9.
Original manuscript agreement between Meredith and Chapman & Hall for the sale to Mssrs. bell & son of the edition of Meredith's works: "Printed for Colonial Circulation only", 1894 November 15.

did.unittitle: Photograph of George Meredith and 3 unidentified women

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by envelope with address in the hand of Meredith to Mrs. Alice Meynell.

did.unittitle: Forman, Maurice Buxton: [The Meredith South African Connexion]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript of an untitled lecture delivered to the Athenaeum Society of South Africa in 1920.

did.unittitle: Forman, Maurice Buxton: George Meredith and the Monthly Observer : 2 sets of proofs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With some manuscript corrections by Forman.

did.unittitle: Meredith, Mary Ellen (Peacock), 1821-1861: Commonplace book of extracts from various authors

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes The Sailor's Consolation , an poem in four stanzas by William Pitt, is copied in George Meredith's early hand. Possibly other notes are in his hand. Undated.

did.unittitle: Meredith, Mary Ellen (Peacock) Nicholls, 1821-1861: The Three Maidens , manuscript poem, possibly by Mary Ellen Meredith
Reich, Jacques (1852-1923): original pencil sketch of George Meredith
Waring, Anna Laetitia (1823-1910): My Times are in Thy Hand, manuscript copy of a poem in the hand of Mary Ellen Meredith
Form of Service for George Meredith's Funeral

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by two blank postcards of George Meredith's residences, as well as an envelope addressed to George Meredith, postmarked 1896 April 22.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous material relating to George Meredith: newspaper clippings, etc.
Gülich, John Perceval, 1865-1899: Illustrations for George Meredith's Lord Ormont and His Aminta .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These illustrations appeared in the Pall Mall Magazine from December 1893 to July 1894.
